After clicking magenta at (8,32):
- White-bordered at (6,17) became gray-bordered at (6,17) (id=6, color=G, pixels=12)
- Gray-bordered at (9,29) became white-bordered at (9,29) (id=13, color=W, pixels=6)
So clicking the orange-bordered magenta at (8,32) swapped the borders of the two gray-bordered objects! The white-bordered moved from (6,17) back to (9,29), and the gray-bordered moved from (9,29) back to (6,17).
This is the reverse of what happened when I clicked gray at (6,17). So clicking magenta at (8,32) undid the previous action!
Wait, but earlier when I clicked magenta at (8,32) (step 4), only 1 cell changed (HUD). Now it swapped borders. The difference is that now white is at (6,17) instead of (9,29).

- Click gray-bordered → white moves to that gray-bordered's position, gray moves to white's old position
- Click magenta → swaps the borders of the two gray-bordered objects (or moves white to the other gray-bordered)

5. Click gray at (6,17) → white moved to (6,17), gray moved to (9,29)
6. Click magenta at (8,32) → white moved to (9,29), gray moved to (6,17)
